prompt:
Design a GreenCycle Warehouse & Content Operations API to run an offline environmental supply warehouse paired with a local publishing and membership program. The backend exposes domain-scoped interface capabilities for Authentication and Roles (local username/password login, session issuance, password rotation), Warehouse Operations (inventory, locations, appointments, outbound waves, and exceptions), Strategy Center (putaway recommendations and pick-path plans), Membership & Billing Ledger (cards/packages and payment records), and CMS (environmental news/policy/education content with taxonomy). Core resource models include Facility, Zone, Location (attributes: type, capacity in cubic feet, hazard class, temperature band, pick-face flag), SKU/Item (ABC class, lot, expiration date, batch, unit weight in lb, unit volume in cu ft), InventoryLot (on-hand, reserved, damaged), Appointment (typed as inbound/outbound with a strict state machine), OutboundOrder (sales/return/transfer), Wave and PickTask, Member and MembershipPackage (punch, term, stored value, bundle), PaymentRecord, Article, Category, and Tag. Appointment APIs must support create/confirm/reschedule/cancel/auto-expire with allowed transitions only; auto-expire triggers if not confirmed within 2 hours of creation, and every transition must persist an immutable operation history record (actor, timestamp, prior/new state, reason). Outbound operations must support wave generation with idempotency keys valid for 24 hours, pick-list creation, optimized pick sequencing, verification, weight/volume capture at pack time (reject if variance exceeds ±5% of expected), outbound handoff recording, and exception handling for stockout/damage/oversell by converting shortages into backorder lines and forcing manager approval to ship partials. The Strategy Center must accept configurable rules (FIFO vs FEFO, ABC velocity weighting, heat level, path cost) and return ranked putaway locations and pick paths; simulation evaluation must score candidate rulesets using the last 30 days of completed pick tasks (locally stored) and output comparative metrics (estimated distance, touches, and constraint violations) without using any external services. CMS APIs must support draft/review/publish/scheduled publish/withdraw with a reviewer role gate; scheduled publish executes on local server time.

The service uses Fastify for the API layer, Prisma for persistence mapping, and SQLite as the single-node datastore to guarantee offline operation in Docker. Data model constraints include unique indexes on username, SKU code, location code, tag normalized name, and invoice number; enums for appointment state, outbound type, package type, and article state; and composite indexes supporting common queries (facility+location, SKU+lot+expiration, member+package status, article publish time). Tagging supports multi-level categories, tag aliases and merges (merged tags remain as tombstones redirecting to the canonical tag), bulk re-association/migration, trending-tag stats computed from the last 7 days of article interactions recorded locally, and tag-cloud export as aggregated counts. Security/compliance capabilities include parameter/dictionary management, API access control via authenticated sessions and role-based authorization, IP allowlists, and rate limiting (default 120 requests/minute per authenticated principal, burst 30). Sensitive fields (password hashes, payment last4, member identifiers) must be encrypted at rest using a locally managed key rotated every 180 days, with masking applied in responses based on role. All create/update operations must emit append-only audit events with before/after digests; destructive actions are soft-deletes with retention of 7 years for billing and 2 years for operational logs. Backup/restore must support encrypted file snapshots to a local filesystem path, and the system must meet p95 response time under 200 ms for typical queries at 50 concurrent requests on a single machine while providing structured logs and health diagnostics without any network dependencies.
